channel.title: Comments for page "Sede Vacante"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Sede Vacante"

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
The image that was used in this article has been replaced with an image that's cc-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) and is sourced as follows:

Title: royal-signature-new.png
Author(s): ANNAfoxlover, Elogee FishTruck
Release year: 2020
Note: Created by Elogee FishTruck as a slightly edited version of the image below.

Source: Wikimedia Commons
License: Public Domain
Title: Foxprint.jpg
Author: ANNAfoxlover

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very well executed. Believable, which is a scary thing to say about a fox requesting that a tiger be ordained pope…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was going to make a John Paul I joke but thought better of it. This is awesome. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
…Matthew should've been bovine. Because then there could've been…

A Papal bull

YEEEEEEEAAAAAH!!!!
*The Who starts playing*

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wonder why they didn't ask for the cardinal to be a cardinal.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, he is a bishop, which is sort of why his name is "Bishop Matthew".

Assuming that the cardinals actually wanted to make him pope, the question of whether the bishopric he now holds is valid could easily be patched by ordaining him bishop of some titular see that's been abandoned for centuries just before ordaining him pope.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
True, but I seemed to recall somewhere that there was some kind of body integrity requirement for Catholic clergy. I must have misremembered.

Anyway, the bigger problem for Matthew is that he's not a bishop, or even a member of the clergy. Apostolic succession, and all that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: *fox religious language

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: At night I sleep on top of a pile up upvotes with many beautiful ladies.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Smapti. What do you do with these upvotes? Is there an upvote bin with a giant S on it? Do you go for a swim in your upvotes sometimes?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Requiring a human as a confessor is still a little strange, though.

Whatever, there is no canon and this tale deserves to exist.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: But but Smapti, you've forgotten the most important question of all: is the tiger a eunuch?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Vatican II is specifically the "certain doctrinal notions" Eugenio mentions that he didn't care for.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Perhaps he lost most of his priesthood during the civil war. Or he's concerned that their not having been trained in a formal seminary means they might be getting some of the details wrong. Or he just wants to cover his bases in case God isn't cool with ducks administering Communion.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
(puts on theologian hat)

Those restrictions only apply to the Jewish temple priesthood (which no longer exists, for the record, since the destruction of the Second Temple and the lack of a replacement means animal sacrifices can't be conducted). Eunuchs and other deformed people are fully qualified to be Catholic priests (Matthew 19:12 For there are eunuchs, who were born so from their mother's womb: and there are eunuchs, who were made so by men: and there are eunuchs, who have made themselves eunuchs for the kingdom of heaven. He that can take, let him take it.) and the testicles thing is just a legend based on certain assumptions about the shape of the papal throne.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Gotta love the Air Bud Defense.

"Ain't no rule says a dog tiger can't play soccer become pope."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ah, but all those injunctions are explicitly with regards to "man" only. It says nothing about the potential disqualifiers for a tiger.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh, gotcha. And good point.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Leviticus 21: 16-24

16 And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,

17 Speak unto Aaron, saying, Whosoever he be of thy seed in their generations that hath any blemish, let him not approach to offer the bread of his God.

18 For whatsoever man he be that hath a blemish, he shall not approach: a blind man, or a lame, or he that hath a flat nose, or any thing superfluous,

19 Or a man that is brokenfooted, or brokenhanded,

20 Or crookbackt, or a dwarf, or that hath a blemish in his eye, or be scurvy, or scabbed, or hath his stones broken;

21 No man that hath a blemish of the seed of Aaron the priest shall come nigh to offer the offerings of the LORD made by fire: he hath a blemish; he shall not come nigh to offer the bread of his God.

22 He shall eat the bread of his God, both of the most holy, and of the holy.

23 Only he shall not go in unto the vail, nor come nigh unto the altar, because he hath a blemish; that he profane not my sanctuaries: for I the LORD do sanctify them.

24 And Moses told it unto Aaron, and to his sons, and unto all the children of Israel.

Supposedly, someone elected to the office of Pope has to have his testicles inspected to confirm the absence of a disability under this provision.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Eugenio et al are aware of Vatican II. This doesn't mean that they agree with it, of course…

Yeah, I recall as much, hence my comment. Even though they know about Vatican II, all of their experience is in the rites and practices from prior to those reforms.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"That's insane! There is no rule that says a tiger can be pope!"

"B-but your holiness, the rules don't say that a tiger can't be pope either!"

Cue Air Bud themesong.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: One drawing of a Bengal tiger in a Pope hat and Popemobile, coming right up.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
DID SOMEONE SAY KNEE-JERK JOHN DIES AT THE END ANALOGY?

Consider that knee moved with such velocity that it has punctured the base of the skull.

In other news, I find this a rather amusing story. I would totally be down for a tiger pope.

e: Curse you Smapti why do I want to feature reference to this in the next Mary-Ann and Salah tale curse you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
EDIT: I made a mistake earlier today. I let a bad day in my personal life cause me to be flippant and react like an entitled brat when I was called out for it.

While I was mulling this over earlier, I realized how much i love this site, and respect the writers here. I have been a member for a while, but I've never contributed as much as I've wanted to, mostly due to time limitations and lack of confidence. I still remember the excitement I felt when my membership was approved, and I hope that when I do find the courage and inspiration to find my writing style and my voice, that I can give back to this community and its members even a fraction of what I've gotten from them.

I apologize for my above comment, Smapti, with the exception that I do think this is well written. And I am sorry if this post was overly sentimental or inappropriate for this discussion page.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
In the comments for 1845, Smapti said that Eugenio et al are aware of Vatican II. This doesn't mean that they agree with it, of course…

And now I kinda want to see a Bengal tiger wearing the Pope hat. Or riding in the Popemobile. Or, well, anything Pope-related :-P

Fun Fact!

It is theoretically possible for a member of the laity, or even a non-Catholic, to be chosen as Pope (although a non-Catholic would need to convert, first). So technically speaking, it isn't completely impossible for a tiger to be chosen.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Recalling my religious background, I don't think I can properly convey my feelings on this without the incoherent noises and facial expressions I made reading through this. To say nothing of the notion of a pre-Vatican-II trained papabili that could literally bite your head off…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have reread this multiple times and love it to pieces.

(but wait I thought 1845-1 requested access to a priest and a seminary, implying he had none of his own)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: but there's no historical Pope Panthera. Leo is the closest they got

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Tales can be funny.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: but he's a tiger, Drew

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh my god, this is fucking brilliant.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
tales being -js

wot u say

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: But it is possible to have something funny that is not a joke.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
smapti pls.

"-j" stands for "joke." Jokes are funny. Therefore, anything that is funny is a joke, and is therefore a "-j"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Considering that those chosen to become Pope take a new name in accordance with the role, I wonder what Bishop Matthew's moniker would be.

I vote for Pope Leo XIV.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Loved it! We should do more stuff like this, an SCP take on current events.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It is our wish to suggest that, when the College of Cardinals convenes presently, that Bishop Matthew the tiger be considered and voted upon to become Pope.

Yesssss. Excellent idea, excellent execution. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Say it with me, kids…

"Just because it's funny doesn't mean it's a -J."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well written, but like all tales involved with 1845, and the skip itself, it comes off as -J material to me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "I'll say! There was this one girl at the Ash Wednesday blessing back in '86 who… wait, that's not what you meant, is it?"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "By the way, Your Holiness, have you ever heard of… Christian foxes?"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'd vote for him.

(If I were a cardinal)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Smapti.

Smapti.

Did you just write a tale about a sapient fox with convictions of grandeur that suggests a tiger would make a super Pope and also satirically pokes at the real world fact that the Catholic Church's administration is culturally and ethnically out of sync with the majority of its congregation? Without breaking the pastiche-y tone with faux religious language or ecclesiastical inaccuracies?

… +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: And now, a tale about SCP-1845 inspired by current events.
